tools.notes: Analyze and categorize the TS from viewpoints influencing design work and planning.
Select overall strategy and partial strategies for important partial systems.
Establish degree of difficulty of the design work.
Establish tasks of operators and individual staff members.
Plan the anticipated design work under time pressure.
Estimate the design costs.
Establish further goals — masters, forms, catalogs.
Improve, optimize — evaluate, select, decide — verify, check, reflect.
Master Gantt chart tracking all engineering phase tasks. Linked to external project management tool.

fields.name: external_gantt_link
fields.label: Link to External Project Planning & Management Tool

tools.notes: P3a tasks: Establish the Transformation Process.
P3b tasks: Establish the TS-Function Structure.
P4 tasks: Establish TS Organ Structure.
P5a tasks: Establish Constructional Structure Part 1.
P5b tasks: Establish Constructional Structure Part 2.
P5.4/P6 tasks: Definitive constructional structure and model decomposition.
Master gating tool for Phase 2. Consolidates all P2.1–P2.8 and Pr2.1/Pr2.2 planning worksheets. Required before engineering analysis (P3) begins.

sections.title: Master Execution Plan
sections.description: Consolidates P2.1–P2.5 and Pr2.2 into a single task register

tools.notes: P3.1: Analyse the TS operational process. Maps input and output operands (Material, Energy, Information, Living Thing) across Human, Technical, Environmental, Information and Management systems.
Alternate transformation process diagram variant A for comparative evaluation.
Alternate transformation process diagram variant B for comparative evaluation.
P3.2: Work out the complete function structure of the TS. Maps functions to parameters, operators and evoked functions. Foundation for morphological matrix in P4.
Function structure variant A for comparative evaluation.
Function structure variant B for comparative evaluation.
P3.3: Represent TS-function structures. Assess and evaluate variants. Output is the optimal function structure that feeds into P4 (organ structure).
P4.1: Enter TS functions from P3.3 into morphological matrix. Find action principles and possible modes of action for each function.
P4.1 Evoked Functions: Establish assisting functions and their organs identified during main morphological analysis.
P4.1 Synthesis: Combine individual organs into a unity. Evaluate organ structure proposals and select the best.
P4.1 Evoked Synthesis: Synthesis of evoked functions and their organs into unified concept.
P4.2: Establish fundamental situations and orders (topology arrangement). In-principle layout showing spatial relationships between organs.
P4.2: Iconic/symbolic topology showing organ layout with more detailed spatial representation.
P4.2: Represent final technical system proposals as organ structures with enough detail for evaluation and selection.
P4.2 Final: Selected optimal organ structure. Conceptual design output that feeds into P5 (constructional design). Must be reviewed and signed off before P5 begins.

tools.tool_title: List of Requirements — Construction Group A
tools.notes: P5a.1.i: Establish and analyze requirements for constructional structure and parts, Group A.

tools.tool_title: List of Requirements — Construction Group B
tools.notes: P5a.1.ii: Establish and analyze requirements for constructional structure and parts, Group B.
P5.2: Establish concept for the constructional structure for each design group. Rough form definition before detailed layout.
P5.3: Establish rough form for each constructional design group. Iterate and optimize toward the definitive structure.
P5.3.1: Represent preliminary layout with dimensions and parts. Feeds into P5.4 for definitive structure.
P5.4: Establish the definitive constructional structure with materials, tolerances, strength analysis, and 3D layout. Direct input into CAD modelling.

P5.4.1: Part-level definitive structure including material spec, strength analysis, CAD file reference, manufacturing notes, tolerance notes and failure modes. One record per major constructional part.
Final tool in WB05. Defines the complete hierarchical parts decomposition (BOM) with iProperty data for CAD modelling. Signing off this tool unlocks WB06 (CAD and Design Engineering).

fields.name: decomposition_complete
fields.label: All parts assigned iProperty data and verified against P5.4.1 sheets?
